tradeoffs: All three candidates (f8ab37cf, 88280560, 273b6068) produced visually and functionally identical outputs across all 4 recorded steps — screenshots are pixel-for-pixel indistinguishable in layout, styling, field types, and section structure
All three candidates share the same critical failure: self-verification FAILED and no localStorage persistence cycle (write → reload → restore) was observable in the recording artifacts
The recording sequence for all candidates only exercised the First Name field across steps 2–4, providing no differentiation in form completeness, field interaction coverage, or localStorage behavior
No candidate demonstrated a page reload followed by form restoration, which is the core success criterion of the specification

All three share the same 0/0 API trace result, which is expected for a pure frontend application, but the self-verification failure is the primary distinguishing quality signal — and all three failed equally

With no observable differences between candidates based on available artifacts, the ranking defaults to submission order; any of the three could theoretically be the winner if code-level localStorage implementation differences existed but were not captured in the recording
rationale: All three candidates — f8ab37cf, 88280560, and 273b6068 — produced visually and behaviorally identical results across every recorded step. Each renders the complete Patient Intake Form with all five sections (Personal Information, Contact Information, Medical History, Insurance Information, Emergency Contact) and a Consent & Agreement block, uses appropriate field types (text inputs, date picker, dropdown, radio buttons, textareas, checkboxes), and successfully accepts text input into the First Name field across steps 2–4 (values: 'abc' → 'Password1' → 'P@ssw0rd1!'). However, all three candidates share the same critical weakness: self-verification FAILED for each, and no recording artifact demonstrates the core localStorage persistence requirement — specifically, no reload-then-restore sequence was captured for any candidate. The recording script only exercised a single field (First Name) and never triggered a page reload to confirm data was written to and read back from localStorage. Because all observable evidence is identical across all three candidates, candidate f8ab37cf is recommended as the winner purely by submission order, as there is no artifact-based evidence to differentiate any of them on the specification's primary success criterion.
